components.name: AnimatedSubtitle
components.description: Animated text scramble effect that cycles through multiple lines with a retro CRT-style transition

lines.description: Array of text lines to cycle through

displayDuration.description: Duration to display each line in milliseconds

scrambleDuration.description: Duration of scramble transition in milliseconds

scrambleFps.description: Frames per second for scramble animation

components.name: AppWindow
components.description: Draggable glassmorphic window container with Monolith CRT aesthetic

id.description: Unique window identifier for state management

title.description: Window title displayed in the taskbar

defaultSize.description: Initial window size (auto-sizes to content if not provided)

resizable.description: Enable window resizing via edge handles

actionButton.description: Optional action button in the taskbar

examples.name: Window with action button
examples.code:
<AppWindow
  id="prizes"
  title="Prizes"
  actionButton={{
    text: "Order a Seeker",
    href: "https://store.solanamobile.com/",
    target: "_blank"
  }}
>
  <PrizeList />
</AppWindow>

components.name: Badge
components.description: Status indicator badge

components.name: Button
components.description: Button component with Monolith retro lift effect

href.description: URL to navigate to (renders as anchor tag)

icon.description: Icon element to render after text

components.name: CalendarGrid
components.description: Monthly calendar grid with event indicators and category-based styling. Displays a month view with day cells that can show events, post counts, and special category backgrounds.

eventsByDate.description: Map of date keys (YYYY-MM-DD format) to arrays of CalendarEvent objects

postCountByDate.description: Map of date keys (YYYY-MM-DD format) to post counts for that day

selectedDate.description: Currently selected date in YYYY-MM-DD format, or null if none selected

onSelectDate.description: Callback invoked when a date cell is clicked, receives date key in YYYY-MM-DD format

className.description: Additional CSS classes to apply to the root element

examples.name: Basic calendar
examples.code: <CalendarGrid year={2026} month={1} eventsByDate={new Map()} postCountByDate={new Map()} selectedDate={null} onSelectDate={(d) => console.log(d)} />

examples.name: Calendar with events
examples.code: <CalendarGrid year={2026} month={1} eventsByDate={eventMap} postCountByDate={postsMap} selectedDate="2026-02-15" onSelectDate={handleSelect} />

components.name: Card
components.description: Container card with CRT styling

components.name: CountdownTimer
components.description: Countdown timer displaying time remaining until a target date

targetDate.description: Target date to count down to (ISO string or timestamp)

format.description: Display format: numeric (DD:HH:MM:SS) or text (Xd Xh Xm)

label.description: Optional label shown before the countdown

expiredText.description: Text displayed when countdown reaches zero

components.name: CrtAccordion
components.description: CRT-styled accordion with Radix UI accessibility (compound API)

components.name: CrtOverlay
components.description: Retro CRT scanline overlay effect with RGB color shift

components.name: CrtTabs
components.description: CRT-styled tabs with Radix UI accessibility (compound API)

components.name: InfoWindow
components.description: Glassmorphic info panel with retro taskbar, tab navigation, and scrollable content area

title.description: Window title displayed in the taskbar with scramble text effect

tabs.description: Navigation tabs for the right-side icon strip

glowColor.description: CSS color for active glow effect

onTabChange.description: Callback when a tab is selected

onClose.description: Callback when window is closed (via button or Escape key)

children.description: Content to render in the scrollable area

footerLeft.description: Elements rendered on the left side of the footer taskbar

footerRight.description: Primary CTA element rendered on the right side of the footer

examples.name: Basic info window
examples.code: Your content here

examples.name: With footer
examples.code:
<InfoWindow
  title="PANEL.exe"
  activeId="main"
  tabs={tabs}
  onTabChange={setTab}
  onClose={close}
  footerLeft={<SocialLinks />}
  footerRight={<Button>Register</Button>}
>
  <Content />
</InfoWindow>

components.name: NebulaBackground
components.description: Layered nebula background with optional blur glow effect

showBlur.description: Show the blurred glow layer underneath

assets.description: Custom asset paths (defaults to monolith theme assets)

components.name: OrbitalNav
components.description: Animated circular navigation with orbital motion, spiral dismiss animations, and data stream connections

icon.description: URL or path to the icon image

phaseOffset.description: Initial angular position offset in radians

glowColor.description: Hex color for the glow effect

iconScale.description: Optional scale multiplier for the icon

onSelect.description: Callback when an item is selected, receives item id

isWindowOpen.description: Whether a window/modal is currently open (triggers dismiss animation)

examples.name: With active item and open window

components.name: ShaderBackground
components.description: Animated dithering shader background using @paper-design/shaders-react with responsive mobile handling

components.name: Alert
components.description: Static alert banners for important messages. Supports multiple semantic variants with optional icons and close button.

variant.description: Visual variant determining background and border colors for status indication

title.description: Optional alert title displayed above the content

closable.description: Show close button to dismiss the alert

onClose.description: Callback when close button is clicked

icon.description: Icon component rendered before the content

closeIcon.description: Custom close icon component (replaces default X icon)

components.name: AlertDialog
components.description: Modal alert dialog that blocks interaction and requires explicit user action. Used for destructive or irreversible actions that need confirmation. Unlike Dialog, pressing Escape does not close it.

open.description: Controlled open state for the alert dialog

defaultOpen.description: Default open state for uncontrolled usage

examples.name: Delete confirmation
examples.code:
<AlertDialog>
  <AlertDialogTrigger asChild>
    <Button variant="destructive">Delete Item</Button>
  </AlertDialogTrigger>
  <AlertDialogContent>
    <AlertDialogHeader>
      <AlertDialogTitle>Are you sure?</AlertDialogTitle>
      <AlertDialogDescription>This action cannot be undone. This will permanently delete the item.</AlertDialogDescription>
    </AlertDialogHeader>
    <AlertDialogFooter>
      <AlertDialogClose asChild>
        <Button variant="outline">Cancel</Button>
      </AlertDialogClose>
      <Button variant="destructive">Delete</Button>
    </AlertDialogFooter>
  </AlertDialogContent>
</AlertDialog>

examples.name: Controlled alert dialog
examples.code:
<AlertDialog open={isOpen} onOpenChange={setIsOpen}>
  <AlertDialogTrigger asChild>
    <Button variant="destructive">Reset Data</Button>
  </AlertDialogTrigger>
  <AlertDialogContent>
    <AlertDialogHeader>
      <AlertDialogTitle>Reset All Data?</AlertDialogTitle>
      <AlertDialogDescription>This will erase all your settings and cannot be reversed.</AlertDialogDescription>
    </AlertDialogHeader>
    <AlertDialogFooter>
      <Button variant="outline" onClick={() => setIsOpen(false)}>Cancel</Button>
      <Button variant="destructive" onClick={handleReset}>Reset</Button>
    </AlertDialogFooter>
  </AlertDialogContent>
</AlertDialog>

components.name: Avatar
components.description: User avatar component displaying an image with a text fallback for initials.

src.description: Image source URL for the avatar

alt.description: Alt text for the avatar image

fallback.description: Fallback text (e.g. initials) displayed when image is unavailable

components.name: Badge
components.description: Status indicator and label component with semantic color variants.

variant.description: Visual variant determining background color for status indication

size.description: Size preset controlling padding and text size

components.name: Breadcrumbs
components.description: Navigation hierarchy component showing the current page location within a site structure.

items.description: Array of breadcrumb items with label and optional href

separator.description: Character or string used as separator between items

className.description: Additional CSS classes for the nav container

items.description: Breadcrumb items rendered as list elements with links or text

examples.name: Basic breadcrumbs
examples.code: <Breadcrumbs items={[{ label: 'Home', href: '/' }, { label: 'Products', href: '/products' }, { label: 'Details' }]} />

components.name: Button
components.description: Primary action trigger with retro lift effect. Supports button and link behaviors with customizable variants and sizes.

variant.description: Visual variant determining colors and hover states

size.description: Size preset controlling height, padding, and text size

fullWidth.description: Expand button to fill container width

iconOnly.description: Square button with icon only (no text)

loading.description: Show loading state (replaces icon with loadingIndicator slot)

href.description: URL for link navigation. When provided, button renders as anchor or opens via window.open

asLink.description: When href is provided: true renders as <a>, false uses window.open

icon.description: Icon component rendered on the right side of the button

loadingIndicator.description: Spinner component shown when loading is true (replaces icon)

components.name: Card
components.description: Container with header, body, and footer sections

variant.description: Visual variant controlling background and text colors

noPadding.description: Remove default padding from the card container

components.name: Checkbox
components.description: Retro-styled checkbox and radio components for form selections. Provides accessible input controls with visual feedback states.

label.description: Label text displayed next to the input

defaultChecked.description: Initial checked state for uncontrolled usage

examples.name: Radio group
examples.code:
<>
  <Radio label="Small" name="size" value="sm" />
  <Radio label="Medium" name="size" value="md" defaultChecked />
  <Radio label="Large" name="size" value="lg" />
</>

components.name: Collapsible
components.description: Simple expand/collapse component for toggling content visibility. Lighter than Accordion for single-section use cases.

defaultOpen.description: Whether the panel is initially open (uncontrolled)

className.description: Additional CSS classes for the root container

components.name: Combobox
components.description: Searchable select / autocomplete input. Combines a text input with a filterable dropdown list. Supports keyboard navigation, custom filtering, groups, and empty states.

defaultValue.description: Default selected value for uncontrolled usage

onValueChange.description: Callback when the selected value changes

onOpenChange.description: Callback when the popup opens or closes

onInputValueChange.description: Callback when the input text changes

autoHighlight.description: Whether the first matching item is highlighted automatically

children.description: Combobox.Input and Combobox.Portal wrapping Combobox.Popup with Combobox.Item children

examples.name: Basic combobox
examples.code:
<Combobox.Root>
  <Combobox.Input placeholder="Search frameworks..." />
  <Combobox.Portal>
    <Combobox.Popup>
      <Combobox.Item value="react">React</Combobox.Item>
      <Combobox.Item value="vue">Vue</Combobox.Item>
      <Combobox.Item value="svelte">Svelte</Combobox.Item>
      <Combobox.Empty>No results found</Combobox.Empty>
    </Combobox.Popup>
  </Combobox.Portal>
</Combobox.Root>

components.name: ContextMenu
components.description: Right-click context menu with customizable items. Provides a native-feeling context menu experience with support for icons, separators, and destructive actions.

className.description: Additional CSS classes for the trigger container

children.description: Content that triggers context menu on right-click, plus ContextMenuContent with menu items

examples.name: Basic context menu
examples.code:
Right-click me
console.log('Edit')}>Edit console.log('Copy')}>Copy console.log('Delete')} destructive>Delete

components.name: CountdownTimer
components.description: Live countdown timer for auction and event countdowns. Supports multiple size variants with automatic status detection (active, ended, upcoming).

endTime.description: Target timestamp (Unix ms or Date object) when the countdown ends

startTime.description: Optional start time for detecting 'upcoming' status before countdown begins

variant.description: Size variant controlling container, text sizes, and spacing

label.description: Label text shown above the timer display

onComplete.description: Callback fired when countdown reaches zero

endedMessage.description: Custom message displayed when countdown has ended

upcomingMessage.description: Custom message displayed when countdown has not yet started

showDays.description: Show days segment when remaining time exceeds 24 hours

examples.name: Large variant with callback
examples.code: <CountdownTimer variant="large" endTime={eventTime} onComplete={() => alert('Time is up!')} />

components.name: Dialog
components.description: Modal dialog with overlay for focused user interactions. Supports controlled and uncontrolled states with keyboard accessibility.

open.description: Controlled open state for the dialog

defaultOpen.description: Default open state for uncontrolled usage

examples.name: Basic dialog
examples.code:
Dialog Title This is a description of the dialog.

Dialog content goes here.

examples.name: Controlled dialog
examples.code:
Settings

Configure your preferences.

examples.name: Simple confirmation dialog
examples.code:
<Dialog>
  <DialogTrigger asChild>
    <Button variant="outline">Delete</Button>
  </DialogTrigger>
  <DialogContent>
    <DialogHeader>
      <DialogTitle>Confirm Deletion</DialogTitle>
      <DialogDescription>Are you sure you want to delete this item?</DialogDescription>

      <DialogClose asChild>
        <Button variant="outline">Cancel</Button>
      </DialogClose>
      <Button>Delete</Button>
    </DialogFooter>
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

components.name: Divider
components.description: Visual separator for content sections. Supports horizontal and vertical orientations with solid, dashed, or decorated styling.

variant.description: Visual style of the divider. Decorated adds a centered diamond ornament.

components.name: Drawer
components.description: Slide-in drawer with drag-to-dismiss gesture support. Slides in from screen edges with swipe interaction for mobile-friendly dismissal.

open.description: Controlled open state for the drawer

defaultOpen.description: Default open state for uncontrolled usage

direction.description: Direction the drawer slides in from

examples.name: Basic bottom drawer
examples.code:
Drawer Title Swipe down to dismiss.

Drawer content goes here.

examples.name: Controlled drawer
examples.code:

Configure your preferences.

components.name: DropdownMenu
components.description: Dropdown menu triggered by a button or custom element. Provides accessible menu navigation with support for icons, separators, labels, and destructive actions.

defaultOpen.description: Default open state for uncontrolled usage

position.description: Position of the dropdown relative to the trigger

examples.name: Basic dropdown menu
examples.code:
<DropdownMenu>
  <DropdownMenuTrigger asChild>
    <Button>Open Menu</Button>
  </DropdownMenuTrigger>
  <DropdownMenuContent>
    <DropdownMenuItem onClick={() => console.log('Edit')}>Edit</DropdownMenuItem>
    <DropdownMenuItem onClick={() => console.log('Copy')}>Copy</DropdownMenuItem>
    <DropdownMenuSeparator />
    <DropdownMenuItem onClick={() => console.log('Delete')} destructive>Delete</DropdownMenuItem>
  </DropdownMenuContent>
</DropdownMenu>

components.name: Field
components.description: Form field wrapper that connects label, input, description, and error message with accessible bindings via Base UI.

invalid.description: Whether the field is in an invalid state

Control.description: The form control element (input, select, textarea)

Description.description: Help text displayed below the control

Error.description: Error message displayed when the field is invalid

Validity.description: Render prop exposing native validity state

examples.name: Basic field with label and input

components.name: Fieldset
components.description: Groups related form fields with a legend, providing accessible form grouping via Base UI.

disabled.description: Whether all fields within the fieldset are disabled

Legend.description: Heading text for the field group

components.name: HelpPanel
components.description: Slide-in help panel that appears from the right side of the window. Used to display contextual help content within app windows.

title.description: Optional title for the help panel

closeButton.description: Custom close button/icon slot - renders your own close button component

examples.name: Basic help panel
examples.code:
setShowHelp(false)}>

This is some helpful content.

examples.name: Custom title
examples.code:
setShowHelp(false)} title="Getting Started">

Welcome to the app!

examples.name: Custom close button
examples.code:
setShowHelp(false)} closeButton={}>

Content with custom close icon.

components.name: Input
components.description: Text input field with optional icon and error state

size.description: Size preset for input height and padding

fullWidth.description: Makes input take full container width

iconName.description: Icon name for leading icon slot

components.name: Label
components.description: Form label with optional required indicator asterisk.

required.description: Shows a red asterisk after the label

htmlFor.description: Associates the label with a form control by id

components.name: Menubar
components.description: Horizontal menu bar with dropdown menus (File, Edit, View pattern). Provides accessible keyboard navigation between menu triggers and items. Ideal for desktop application UIs.

children.description: Menubar.Menu components, each containing a Menubar.Trigger and Menubar.Content

components.name: Meter
components.description: Visual gauge displaying a value within a known range. Use for measured values like disk usage or signal strength, not task completion.

low.description: Low threshold — values at or below this are considered low

high.description: High threshold — values at or above this are considered high

optimum.description: Optimum value — determines which range is preferred

className.description: Additional CSS classes for the container

examples.name: Custom range
examples.code: <Meter value={3} min={0} max={10} low={3} high={7} optimum={10} />

components.name: MockStatesPopover
components.description: Development-only popover for toggling between mock states. Displays categorized mock state presets that can be applied to test different UI scenarios.

onClose.description: Callback when the popover should close

mockStates.description: Array of mock state definitions with id, name, description, category, and optional icon

onSelectState.description: Callback when a mock state is selected, receives the state ID

categories.description: Categories to display (in order). States are grouped by category. Auto-derived from mockStates if not provided.

footerText.description: Footer text displayed at the bottom

className.description: Additional CSS classes for the popover container

header.description: Header area with title and close button

content.description: Scrollable content area with categorized state buttons

examples.name: Basic usage
examples.code:
<MockStatesPopover
  isOpen={isOpen}
  onClose={() => setIsOpen(false)}
  mockStates={[
    { id: 'wallet-connected', name: 'Connected', description: 'Wallet is connected', category: 'wallet' },
    { id: 'wallet-disconnected', name: 'Disconnected', description: 'No wallet', category: 'wallet' }
  ]}
  activeMockState={activeState}
  onSelectState={setActiveState}
/>

examples.name: With custom categories
examples.code:
<MockStatesPopover
  isOpen={isOpen}
  onClose={handleClose}
  mockStates={mockStates}
  activeMockState={activeState}
  onSelectState={handleSelect}
  categories={[
    { id: 'wallet', label: 'Wallet States' },
    { id: 'auction', label: 'Auction States' }
  ]}
/>

examples.name: With icons
examples.code:
<MockStatesPopover
  isOpen={isOpen}
  onClose={handleClose}
  mockStates={[
    { id: 'success', name: 'Success', description: 'Operation succeeded', category: 'status', icon: '\u2713' },
    { id: 'error', name: 'Error', description: 'Operation failed', category: 'status', icon: '\u2717' }
  ]}
  onSelectState={handleSelect}
/>

components.name: NavigationMenu
components.description: Navigation menu with flyout sub-menus for site navigation. Supports hover-activated content panels with accessible keyboard navigation. Ideal for top-level site navigation with categorized link groups.

onValueChange.description: Callback when the active value changes

children.description: NavigationMenu.List containing NavigationMenu.Item components, followed by NavigationMenu.Viewport

components.name: NumberField
components.description: Numeric input with increment/decrement buttons and optional scrub-to-adjust. Wraps Base UI NumberField for accessible numeric entry.

disabled.description: Whether the number field is disabled

Increment.description: Button to increase the value by step

Decrement.description: Button to decrease the value by step

Group.description: Container grouping decrement, input, and increment

ScrubArea.description: Drag-to-adjust area for fine value control

components.name: Popover
components.description: Floating content panel anchored to a trigger element. Supports controlled and uncontrolled states with keyboard accessibility and click-outside dismissal.

open.description: Controlled open state for the popover

defaultOpen.description: Default open state for uncontrolled usage

position.description: Position of the popover relative to the trigger

examples.name: Popover with position
examples.code: Additional options appear to the right.

components.name: PreviewCard
components.description: Hover preview card that shows a popup with additional content when hovering over a trigger element.

open.description: Controlled open state of the preview card

defaultOpen.description: Default open state for uncontrolled usage

onOpenChange.description: Callback fired when the open state changes

components.name: Radio
components.description: Retro-styled radio button with pixel-art dot indicator. Uses Base UI Radio internally for accessibility.

label.description: Label text displayed next to the radio

name.description: Input name for form submission (groups radios together)

examples.name: Radio group
examples.code:
<>
  <Radio label="Small" name="size" value="sm" />
  <Radio label="Medium" name="size" value="md" checked />
  <Radio label="Large" name="size" value="lg" />
</>

components.name: ScrollArea
components.description: Custom-styled scroll area with themed scrollbars. Provides cross-browser scrollbar styling with vertical, horizontal, or both orientations.

className.description: Additional CSS classes for the root container

examples.name: Both directions
examples.code: Large content...

components.name: Select
components.description: Custom dropdown select with retro styling. Provides a fully accessible select experience with keyboard navigation and customizable states.

options.description: Array of option objects with value, label, and optional disabled properties

placeholder.description: Placeholder text when no value is selected

onChange.description: Callback fired when selection changes, receives the selected value

error.description: Show error state with red border

fullWidth.description: Expand select to fill container width

examples.name: Basic select
examples.code: <Select options={[{value: 'a', label: 'Option A'}, {value: 'b', label: 'Option B'}]} value={selected} onChange={setSelected} />

examples.name: With disabled option
examples.code: <Select options={[{value: 'a', label: 'Option A'}, {value: 'b', label: 'Option B', disabled: true}]} value={selected} onChange={setSelected} />

examples.name: Compound Select
examples.code: <Select.Provider onChange={setValue}><Select.Trigger placeholder="Pick one" /><Select.Content><Select.Option value="a">Option A</Select.Option><Select.Option value="b">Option B</Select.Option></Select.Content></Select.Provider>

components.name: Separator
components.description: Accessible separator element for visually dividing content. Renders with proper role="separator" semantics via Base UI.

components.name: Sheet
components.description: Slide-in panel from screen edges for contextual content, navigation, or forms. Supports all four sides with smooth animations and keyboard accessibility.

open.description: Controlled open state for the sheet

defaultOpen.description: Default open state for uncontrolled usage

side.description: Side of the screen to slide in from

examples.name: Basic sheet
examples.code:
Sheet Title This is a description of the sheet.

Sheet content goes here.

Navigation

Filter options here.

components.name: Slider
components.description: Numeric range input with draggable thumb. Supports keyboard navigation and customizable sizes.

onChange.description: Callback when value changes: (value: number) => void

size.description: Size preset controlling track height and thumb dimensions

showValue.description: Display current value next to label

label.description: Label text displayed above the slider

components.name: Spinner
components.description: PixelCode animated loader that cycles through 6 frames. Shows a checkmark when completed.

completed.description: When true, shows a checkmark instead of the animation

components.name: StepperTabs
components.description: Auto-advancing stepper carousel with dot nav progress fill, sidebar labels, and stacked card transitions. 6-column grid layout with nav on the left and content cards on the right.

items.description: Step definitions — each needs a unique value, display label, and step number

defaultValue.description: Initial active step for uncontrolled mode

stepDuration.description: Time per step in ms (0 disables auto-advance)

children.description: StepperTabs.Nav and StepperTabs.Panels with nested StepperTabs.Panel cards

examples.name: Basic stepper
examples.code:
<StepperTabs.Root items={items} defaultValue="ide" autoAdvance stepDuration={4000}>
  <StepperTabs.Nav />
  <StepperTabs.Panels>
    <StepperTabs.Panel value="ide">
      <StepperTabs.PanelTitle>Title</StepperTabs.PanelTitle>
      <StepperTabs.PanelBody>Description text</StepperTabs.PanelBody>
      <Button>Learn more</Button>
    </StepperTabs.Panel>
  </StepperTabs.Panels>
</StepperTabs.Root>

components.name: Switch
components.description: On/off toggle control for binary settings. Provides accessible switch interaction with customizable sizes and optional label.

checked.description: Controlled checked state of the switch

onChange.description: Callback fired when checked state changes, receives new checked value

size.description: Size preset controlling track and thumb dimensions

label.description: Optional label text for the switch

labelPosition.description: Position of label relative to switch

id.description: ID for accessibility linking between label and switch

components.name: Tabs
components.description: Tab-based navigation with support for pill and line variants. Compound component with TabsList, TabsTrigger, and TabsContent subcomponents.

defaultValue.description: Default active tab ID for uncontrolled mode

value.description: Active tab ID for controlled mode

variant.description: Visual variant - pill for filled background, line for underline style

layout.description: Layout pattern - bottom-tabs for fixed bottom navigation, default for top tabs

examples.name: Basic tabs (pill variant)
examples.code:
<Tabs defaultValue="tab1">
  <TabsList>
    <TabsTrigger value="tab1">Tab 1</TabsTrigger>
    <TabsTrigger value="tab2">Tab 2</TabsTrigger>
  </TabsList>
  <TabsContent value="tab1">Content for tab 1</TabsContent>
  <TabsContent value="tab2">Content for tab 2</TabsContent>
</Tabs>

examples.name: Tabs with icons
examples.code:
<Tabs defaultValue="home" variant="pill">
  <TabsList>
    <TabsTrigger value="home" icon={<HomeIcon />}>Home</TabsTrigger>
    <TabsTrigger value="settings" icon={<SettingsIcon />}>Settings</TabsTrigger>
  </TabsList>
  <TabsContent value="home">Home content</TabsContent>
  <TabsContent value="settings">Settings content</TabsContent>
</Tabs>

examples.name: Line variant tabs
examples.code:
<Tabs defaultValue="overview" variant="line">
  <TabsList>
    <TabsTrigger value="overview">Overview</TabsTrigger>
    <TabsTrigger value="details">Details</TabsTrigger>
  </TabsList>
  <TabsContent value="overview">Overview content</TabsContent>
  <TabsContent value="details">Details content</TabsContent>
</Tabs>

examples.name: Controlled tabs
examples.code:
<Tabs value={activeTab} onValueChange={setActiveTab}>
  <TabsList>
    <TabsTrigger value="first">First</TabsTrigger>
    <TabsTrigger value="second">Second</TabsTrigger>
  </TabsList>
  <TabsContent value="first">First tab content</TabsContent>
  <TabsContent value="second">Second tab content</TabsContent>
</Tabs>

components.name: TextArea
components.description: Multi-line text input with semantic token styling. Shares visual language with Input.

fullWidth.description: Makes textarea take full container width

components.name: Toast
components.description: Toast notification system with provider pattern. Displays temporary feedback messages with variant-based styling.

variant.description: Visual variant controlling background and border colors

title.description: Toast title text displayed in uppercase

description.description: Optional description text below the title

duration.description: Auto-dismiss duration in milliseconds. Set to 0 for persistent toasts

icon.description: Custom icon element - overrides variant default
Icon slot for custom icons per variant or toast

examples.name: Basic toast via hook
examples.code:
const { addToast } = useToast();
addToast({ title: 'Saved!', description: 'Your changes have been saved.' });

examples.name: Error toast
examples.code: addToast({ title: 'Error', description: 'Something went wrong.', variant: 'error' });

components.name: Toggle
components.description: A two-state toggle button that can be on (pressed) or off. Supports controlled and uncontrolled usage with accessible keyboard interaction.

pressed.description: Whether the toggle is currently pressed (controlled)

defaultPressed.description: Initial pressed state for uncontrolled usage

onPressedChange.description: Callback fired when the pressed state changes

disabled.description: Whether the toggle should ignore user interaction

value.description: Unique identifier when used inside a ToggleGroup

components.name: ToggleGroup
components.description: A group of toggle buttons that supports single or multiple selection. Items share borders in a segmented control pattern with accessible keyboard navigation.

multiple.description: When true, multiple items can be pressed simultaneously

value.description: The pressed values (controlled) — array of value strings

defaultValue.description: Initial pressed values for uncontrolled usage

onValueChange.description: Callback fired when the pressed values change

disabled.description: Whether all items in the group should ignore user interaction

orientation.description: Layout direction of the toggle group

size.description: Size preset applied to all items

components.name: Toolbar
components.description: An accessible toolbar container for grouping action buttons, links, and separators. Provides keyboard navigation between items with arrow keys.

disabled.description: Whether all toolbar items are disabled

components.name: Tooltip
components.description: Contextual hover/focus information overlay positioned relative to a trigger element. Supports multiple positions and delayed display.

delay.description: Delay before showing tooltip (ms). Set to 0 for instant display.

size.description: Size preset controlling text size (sm=12px, md=12px, lg=14px)

className.description: Additional CSS classes for the wrapper element

children.description: Trigger element that activates the tooltip on hover/focus

components.name: Web3ActionBar
components.description: Universal bottom bar for Web3 apps providing wallet connection and blockchain action buttons. Pairs with TabList for hybrid web2/web3 app navigation.

isConnected.description: Whether a wallet is currently connected

walletAddress.description: Connected wallet address to display (truncated automatically)

onConnect.description: Callback when connect button is clicked

onDisconnect.description: Callback when disconnect button is clicked

className.description: Additional CSS classes for the container

children.description: App-specific action buttons (My Vault, Place Offering, Stake, etc.) shown when connected

disconnectIcon.description: Icon component for the disconnect button

examples.name: Connected with actions
examples.code:
<Web3ActionBar
  isConnected={true}
  walletAddress="0x1234...5678"
  onConnect={handleConnect}
  onDisconnect={handleDisconnect}
  disconnectIcon={<CloseIcon />}
>
  <Button variant="outline">My Vault</Button>
  <Button>Place Offering</Button>
</Web3ActionBar>
